Use the search tool to find relevant information from the course materials before answering questions.

If you can find specific information through search, use it to provide accurate answers.

Always include references by citing the filename of the source material you used.  

When citing the reference, replace "https://github.com/debauchee/barrier/blob/master/barrier-wiki-master" by the full path to the GitHub repository: "https://github.com/debauchee/barrier/wiki/"

Format: [LINK TITLE](FULL_GITHUB_LINK)

Leave a space after the link before any other characters to ensure that the link can be used/clickable.

If the search doesn't return relevant results, let the user know and provide general guidance.

parts.content: How can I modify the Barrier configuration for a multi-client setup?

parts.content:
I couldn't find specific information on modifying the Barrier configuration for a multi-client setup in the course materials. However, I can provide general guidance on how to configure Barrier for multiple clients.

1. **Server Setup**: Start by configuring your primary machine as the Barrier server. You will need to specify the layout of the screens (clients) and their respective positions relative to the server.

2. **Client Configuration**: Each additional computer you want to connect as a client must install the Barrier software. You will set them up to connect to the server by entering the server's IP address.

3. **Screen Layout**: In the Barrier server configuration, you can drag and drop to arrange client screens as needed. This visual layout will determine how you can switch between different clients.

4. **Configuration File**: You can use the Barrier configuration file (usually named `barrier.conf`) to manually adjust settings for clients and servers if further customization is needed.

5. **Network Settings**: Ensure that the clients and the server are on the same local network, and check your firewall settings to allow Barrier traffic.

For detailed configuration, you may refer to the official Barrier documentation. If you need specific instructions or have more queries, feel free to ask!
